title: Laplace Transforms: Derivatives, IVPs, Unit Step, Shifting, and Convolution

frontMatter.title: Laplace Transforms: Derivatives, IVPs, Unit Step, Shifting, and Convolution
frontMatter.summary: This lecture covers Laplace transform properties for derivatives and integrals, unit step functions, shifting theorems, solving initial value problems (IVPs), and the convolution theorem.
table_of_contents: IVPs with Piecewise Continuous Forcing Functions
Derivative and Integration of Laplace Transform
Laplace Transform of Functions Divided by t

key_formulas.name: Laplace Transform of First Derivative
key_formulas.note: Relates the transform of a derivative to the transform of the function and its initial value.

key_formulas.name: Laplace Transform of k-th Derivative
key_formulas.note: General formula for the Laplace transform of higher-order derivatives.

key_formulas.name: Unit Step Function
key_formulas.note: Defines a jump discontinuity at $t=a$, useful for modeling switching behavior.

key_formulas.name: Laplace Transform of Unit Step Function
key_formulas.note: The transform of the unit step function.

key_formulas.name: Second Shifting Theorem (t-shift)
key_formulas.note: Translates the time axis for the function $f(t)$.

key_formulas.name: Second Shifting Theorem (s-shift)
key_formulas.note: Alternative form useful when the function is not in $f(t-a)$ form.

key_formulas.name: Laplace Transform of Multiplication by t
key_formulas.note: Relates multiplication by $t$ in time domain to differentiation in s-domain.

key_formulas.name: Laplace Transform of Division by t
key_formulas.note: Relates division by $t$ in time domain to integration in s-domain.

key_formulas.name: Convolution Integral
key_formulas.note: Defines the convolution of two functions, fundamental for the convolution theorem.

key_formulas.name: Convolution Theorem
key_formulas.note: The Laplace transform of a convolution is the product of their individual Laplace transforms.
